subject: Anthropology
title: Stateless Societies
description: A short lesson to help you apply Stateless Societies.

hook.text: No president, no police, no courts. Yet millions of people governed themselves effectively without any state at all.

buildup.text: Stateless societies maintain order through kinship, custom, reciprocity, and informal social pressure instead of law.

discovery.text: Evans-Pritchard studied the Nuer, who resolved disputes through lineage systems, not courts or formal authority.

twist.text: Stateless doesn't mean chaotic. These societies often have rich systems for peace, justice, and decision-making.

climax.text: Stateless societies prove that humans don't need centralized government. Order can emerge from relationships alone.

punchline.text: You don't need a state to have a society.

quiz.question: How do stateless societies maintain order?
options: Through kinship, custom, and social pressure
